title: MWL workflow definition
description: The meta-schema for a Metolia Workflow Language (MWL) workflow definition: a JSON document whose root is a Flow object. The prose specification is normative; this schema is its machine-checkable companion, and a definition MAY be validated against it. Cross-field constraints the prose states (entrypoint and next resolving to steps keys, flow names resolving to flows entries, provider URIs resolving against a catalog) are not expressible here and remain the prose's alone.

$schema.description: Identifies the document as a Flow definition and the spec version it is authored against. Root document only.

flowFields.description: The fields common to every Flow object, wherever it appears: the root document, a named flows entry, or an inline call.flow value.

entrypoint.description: The Step where execution enters the graph. MUST be a key of this Flow's steps object.

flows.description: A map from name to Flow object: named subflows a call can target by name.

middleware.description: Ordered middleware wrapping the Flow's Step graph, outside-in: the first entry is the outermost wrapper.

parameters.description: A JSON Schema (2020-12) document describing the Flow's named parameters; what a caller's with is validated against.

steps.description: A map from Step name to Step definition: the Flow's Step graph.

flow.description: A Flow object in a non-root position: a named flows entry or an inline call.flow value. Same fields and semantics as the root, without $schema.

step.description: A Step definition: an action discriminator with the fields that action accepts.

stepCall.description: A Call Step: dispatches a single call object and routes on the Result it yields.

input.description: Shapes the value entering the Step's machinery. Default: {{ step.input }}.

output.description: Shapes the value the Step emits on success. Default: {{ step.result.value }}.

middleware.description: Ordered middleware wrapping the Step's dispatch, outside-in.

stepGather.description: A Gather Step: fans dispatches out concurrently and gathers the Results back in. Carries exactly one of its two forms: over with call (iterate), or calls (scatter).

over.description: Iterate form: the collection to dispatch over, one dispatch per element. MUST produce an array.

call.description: Iterate form: the one call template, dispatched once per element of the over result.

calls.description: Scatter form: a literal, non-empty array of call objects, one dispatch per entry.

concurrency.description: Caps the number of dispatches active at once. A literal positive integer, or null for unlimited; no expression. Default (absent or null): unlimited.

output.description: Shapes the value the Step emits on success. Default: the success projection over step.results.

completion.description: A Gather's completion policy. Absent, every dispatch must succeed.

successes.description: The number of dispatches that must succeed, evaluated once after the dispatches are enumerated.

wait.description: Whether dispatches still pending once the outcome is determined run to completion (true) or are cancelled and skipped (false). A literal; no expression.

stepMatch.description: A Match Step: routes to one of several successors by testing predicates against a single shaped value.

input.description: Shapes the value the clause predicates test, read by every clause as match.input. Default: {{ step.input }}.

cases.description: Ordered clauses; the first whose when holds is selected, and no later predicate evaluates.

matchCase.description: A cases clause: a predicate with the routing, shaping, and capture that apply when it is selected.

when.description: The clause's predicate. A when that fails to evaluate does not propagate: the clause simply does not match.

output.description: Shapes the value the clause's next receives. Default: {{ match.input }}.

matchDefault.description: The required fallback clause, selected when no case matches: the case shape without when.

output.description: Shapes the value the clause's next receives. Default: {{ match.input }}.

stepPass.description: A Pass Step: performs no action work; emits a value and captures variables, then transitions.

output.description: Shapes the value the Step emits. Default: {{ step.input }}.

stepSleep.description: A Sleep Step: pauses the frame for a duration or until an instant. Exactly one of for and until MUST be present.

for.description: An ISO 8601 duration, or an expression producing one.

until.description: An RFC 3339 timestamp, or an expression producing one.

stepReturn.description: A Return Step: the terminal success. The frame completes with a success Result carrying the shaped value.

value.description: Shapes the value the success Result carries. Default: {{ step.input }}.

stepRaise.description: A Raise Step: the terminal failure. With a result, constructs the failure; bare, re-raises the failure being handled.

raiseResult.description: The authorable fields of the failure envelope. Each field accepts an expression; unwritten fields are absent.

type.description: The failure Result's type. MUST be a non-success type. Default: "error".

code.description: The specific failure identifier: a dotted string whose first segment is, by convention, a namespace.

retryable.description: An advisory retry signal: true, false, or null (no assertion).

previous.description: A chained prior failure this one supersedes. Unwritten, the engine links any active failure; null severs the chain.

call.description: A call object: a request to run a target and obtain its Result. MUST name exactly one target, provider or flow.

flow.description: A named Flow declared in flows, referenced by bare name, or an inline Flow object.

input.description: A data payload threaded into the target; a separate channel from with. Default: {{ call.input }}.

with.description: Arguments supplied to the target, validated against the target's parameters schema.

onSuccess.description: The success arm: shapes the value placed in the Call's success Result and captures variables.

value.description: Produces the value the Call's success Result carries. Default: {{ call.result.value }}.

onFailure.description: The failure arm: captures variables from the failed dispatch's live context. It does not reshape the failure.

middleware.description: An ordered array of middleware entries, outside-in: the first entry is the outermost wrapper.

middlewareEntry.description: A middleware entry: names a middleware by provider URI and configures its participation phase by phase. An absent phase block is equivalent to an empty one.

phaseOnEntry.description: Configures the entry's onEntry phase, run once on the way down at first entry.

output.description: Shapes the value passed down to the next inner entry or, at the last entry, into the wrapped operation. Default: {{ middleware.input }}.

phaseOnSuccess.description: Configures the entry's onSuccess phase, run on ascent when the rising Result is a success.

value.description: Produces the success value the entry emits upward. Default: {{ middleware.result.value }}.

phaseOnFailure.description: Configures the entry's onFailure phase, run on ascent when the rising Result is a failure. Writing any envelope field constructs a new failure superseding the rising one; an unwritten field other than previous is taken from the superseded failure.

type.description: The constructed failure's type. MUST be a non-success type.

retryable.description: The constructed failure's advisory retry signal.

previous.description: Overrides the engine's chain link. Typically written only to sever the chain with null.

phaseOnAlways.description: Configures the entry's onAlways phase, run on every ascent after onSuccess or onFailure. The phase has no shaping key: the rising Result passes through unchanged.

when.description: A predicate gating the phase's action: when false, the action does not run and the phase's with is not evaluated. Author shaping always evaluates. Default: true.

phaseWith.description: The middleware's parameter namespace for the phase, validated against the schema its contract declares for that phase. Either an object whose fields each accept an expression, or a single expression producing the whole object.

with.description: Arguments supplied to a call's target. Either an object whose fields each accept an expression, or a single expression producing the whole object.

catch.description: The Step's failure-path routing: ordered clauses, the first whose match matches the failure wins.

catchClause.description: A conditional failure edge: when the Step's failure matches, control transitions to the clause's next.

output.description: Shapes the value the handler Step receives. Default: {{ step.input }}.

failureMatcher.description: A failure matcher: each member constrains one contract field of the failure envelope, and every member present must match. Structural; member values are data, not expressions.

codes.description: Failure-code patterns: "Prefix.Code" (exact), "Prefix.*" (prefix), or "*" (any failure). Matches when the failure's code matches any pattern.

types.description: Non-success Result type names. Matches when the failure's type is any of them; "success" is not permitted.

retryable.description: Matches only a failure asserting the same explicit retryable value; an unset signal (absent or null) matches neither.

assign.description: A map from variable name to expression; each expression's value is bound to that name in vars on successful exit. Every expression in the block evaluates against the variable state from before the block.

stepName.description: A Step reference. MUST be a key of the same steps map; cross-scope transitions are not permitted.

callProviderUri.description: A call-provider URI under the mwl scheme: mwl:provider.call/<namespace>/<name...>. Segments MUST NOT be "." or "..".

middlewareProviderUri.description: A middleware-provider URI under the mwl scheme: mwl:provider.middleware/<namespace>/<name...>. Segments MUST NOT be "." or "..".

parametersSchema.description: A JSON Schema (2020-12) document describing named parameters. MUST have "type": "object" at the top level. Validation against it is closed by default: absent additionalProperties is evaluated as false.

expression.description: An expression: a string whose entire content one delimiter pair sets off. This version defines one pair, {{ }}, identifying CEL.
